sections.paragraph:
m. and נְתִיבָה f. ־ִים and ־ֹות.-

(1) prop. adj. trodden with the feet (see the root נָתַב ). דֶּרֶךְ נְתִיבָה a trodden way, Pro 12:28 and without דֶּרֶךְ id. Jud. 5:6 , and hence-

(2) a footpath, by-way, a poetic word, Job 18:10 28:7 41:24 . Plur. נְתִיבֹות בֵּיתֹו the ways to his house, Job 38:20.
